description: Build a static Ink renderer for shared compact terminal rows with terminal-safe output and tests.

userStories.title: Add Ink renderer module skeleton and dependency decision
userStories.description: As a CLI implementer, I want an Ink renderer module skeleton so terminal presentation can be developed separately from Web UI and session runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Document the selected Ink package/version or add an implementation note if dependency addition is deferred
Create CLI renderer module paths for Ink terminal view, row, line, colors, markdown, and JSON helpers
Renderer modules do not import Web DOM components or Web-only libraries
userStories.notes: Depends on shared terminal view-model boundary.

userStories.title: Implement Ink row and line rendering
userStories.description: As an SSH operator, I want compact terminal rows to render in my shell with readable symbols and colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement InkTerminalView over shared compact rows with a bounded row window option
Implement InkTerminalRow and InkTerminalLine using Ink Box/Text primitives
Map user, assistant, tool, yielded run, and error rows to terminal-safe markers and colors
Renderer handles unknown or missing row fields without crashing

userStories.title: Implement terminal-safe markdown and JSON helpers
userStories.description: As a terminal user, I want rich content to display as bounded readable terminal text instead of browser HTML widgets.
acceptanceCriteria: Markdown helper renders paragraphs, lists, code fences, and links as terminal text
JSON helper pretty-prints bounded values and marks truncation
Helpers avoid browser markdown, Prism, and interactive JSON tree dependencies
Tests cover markdown links/code blocks and large JSON truncation

userStories.title: Add Ink render-to-string tests
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want deterministic renderer tests so terminal row output regressions are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: Add renderToString or equivalent tests for representative compact row fixtures
Tests verify output contains expected markers for user, assistant, tool, yielded run, and error rows
Tests verify large row lists use the bounded window behavior
